chunks.title: Course Information
chunks.content:
Course Number: ISEN 210
Course Title: Deterministic Optimization Model Design
Meeting Location: ETB 1034
Credit Hours: 2
Website: Canvas will contain announcements and other course information. Specifically, Canvas will be used to distribute class materials (homework assignments, notes, and grades). Please check Canvas regularly: any information posted on it will be as valid as if it were mentioned in class.
Email: I use email extensively to send announcements and important reminders. Students are expected to read all emails in their entirety. I aim to respond to all student emails within 24 hours. However, my inbox exploded, so I occasionally miss emails. If I have not responded to an email within 24 hours of its send time, feel free to send me a reminder.

chunks.title: Course Description
chunks.content: Development and application of fundamental deterministic optimization models; emphasis on formulating real-world problems as linear, integer, and network flow models; computer optimization software will be used to solve the linear and integer models.

chunks.title: Course Prerequisites
chunks.content: Grade of C or better in MATH 152 and ENGR 102.

chunks.title: Learning Outcomes
chunks.content:
At the end of the course, students should be able to:
• Quantitative Modeling: Gain expertise in constructing and formulating quantitative models specific to linear programming, integer programming, and network flow problems.
• Analytical Problem-Solving: Analyze complex real-world scenarios to evaluate the suitability of linear programming as a modeling technique for strategic decision-making.
• Real-world Model Development: Design comprehensive linear, integer, and network flow models by integrating essential components and variables of complex real-world issues.
• Proficiency in Optimization Software: Achieve proficiency in utilizing state-of-the-art computer optimization software tools to model and resolve practical problems accurately.

chunks.title: Course Expectations
chunks.content:
In this course, students are expected to:
• Be respectful
• Do all homework
• Participate in class
• Ask any questions and doubts as soon as they arise (i.e., be proactive)

chunks.title: Textbook and/or Resource Materials
chunks.content:
Required Textbook:
• [OPTION 1] Introduction to Mathematical Programming: Applications and Algorithms, Volume 1, 4th Edition; W. L. Winston (and M. Venkataraman); Duxbury Press, CA. (ISBN 0534359647).
• [OPTION 2] Operations research: applications and algorithms, 4th Edition; Winston, Wayne L. (and Jeffrey B. Goldberg); Thomson/Brooks/Cole. (ISBN 9780534380588)
• IMPORTANT: The second textbook is a word-for-word superset of the first one. Both books are out of print, but used copies abound. The book's first three chapters will be available through Canvas, giving students three weeks to buy a used copy of one of the textbooks.
Additional references:
• Student solutions manual for the required textbook (available at Canvas)
• AMPL: A Modeling Language for Mathematical Programming. 2nd Edition, Fourer, et al., Duxbury Press, 2002. (ISBN 0-534-38809-4) Free at http://www.ampl.com/BOOK/download.html
Copies of the textbook and references are available on reserve in the library.
Course Reserves are on the first floor of the Evans Library Annex Lobby at the front desk.

chunks.title:
Grading Policy
Homework
chunks.content:
Homework will not be graded; solutions will be handed out for self-check. I strongly suggest that you do the homework (this is the only way to learn this material) and that you follow this strategy for doing the homework:`
1. Try * hard* to solve the problems by yourself (do *not* look at the solution yet)
2. Work in groups to solve the problems you were not able to solve or are not sure about the solution (do *not* look at the solution yet)
3. First by yourself and then in groups:
For each of the problems:
a) Check your solution for the problem against the solution provided; if it is different, try to understand it thoroughly. IMPORTANT: Do NOT look at solutions to the other problems.
b) With the knowledge gained from 3a, review all your other solutions and correct them if needed.

chunks.title: AI BONUS POINTS OPPORTUNITY
chunks.content:
1. For the modeling problems, solve each of the homework problems using Al and compare the Al solutions to your solution and to the solution provided.
2. If for any homework problem, the solution provided by the Al is 100% correct---either “as is” or including corrections made by you--- AND it is substantially different from “my" solution (i.e., the differences are far more contrived than minor variable changes or units), then
a) Send me such a solution via email along with all the processes you followed to obtain it (i.e., all the chat transcripts and all your corrections to the Al solution, if any).
b) *IF* it is 100% correct AND *IF* I determine your solution is truly substantially different, then I will give you a bonus on the related exam (LP, IP, or Final). The amount and type of bonus points will be determined after each exam; they will be generous but depend on the number of attempts.
HOWEVER, to disincentivize students from submitting anything and everything that Al yields without a proper understanding of the correctness or lack thereof of the Al output, *IF* the submitted solution is not correct or if the solution is only trivially different to “my” solution, then that student will be forbidden from attempting again obtaining these "Al bonus points" until after the subsequent major exam. To avoid collaboration in this bonus mechanism, only one student (the one submitting the first correct, substantially different solution) may receive bonus points per homework problem.

chunks.title: Quizzes
chunks.content: There will be approximately weekly quizzes on the previous week's material (lecture, readings, and homework).

chunks.title: Grading Weights
chunks.content: 25% LP Exam, 25% IP Exam, 10% AMPL Exam, 10% Quizzes, and 30% Final Exam.

chunks.title: Scale Grading
chunks.content: Final grades will be based on this scale: A: [90-100], B: [80-90), C: [70-80), D: [60-70), F: [0,60)

chunks.title: Final Grade Policy
chunks.content: I never have extra projects or individual deals to improve grades. I do my best throughout the semester to help you learn the material, but by the time it's close to the final or after it, it is too late. At that time, everything in the course is said and done, and you earned what you earned. Therefore, please refrain from asking for help with the grade; giving you such help would be unfair to everyone else who is not asking for or getting a "deal" from me. I provide several redemption opportunities throughout the semester, but none just before and after the final. Please save us an uncomfortable conversation where all I can do is cry with you, but at the end of the day, I will submit the grade you earned and nothing else.

chunks.title: Re-grading Policy and Procedure
chunks.content:
Time limit: One week after any graded work is released.
Format: Regrade requests must be made through GradeScope.
Required Contents:
1. State precisely what you think was misgraded (e.g., the relevant problem subsection).
2. State specifically why you think it is correct (i.e., clearly justify why regrading is warranted).
3. State explicitly: “I understand that this regrade request may result in further point deductions if new errors are discovered." (i.e., COPY-PASTE the underlined sentence in your regrade request; otherwise, your request will be denied).

chunks.title: Attendance & Class Participation
chunks.content:
• Students must attend class in person on the days when we will have the weekly quizzes (all of which are announced in advance); likewise, students must attend in person to take the exams.
• Participation in class is highly encouraged and appreciated, but is not graded or mandatory.

chunks.title: Required Documentation of Absences (for missed quizzes and exams)
chunks.content:
The student must provide satisfactory evidence to the instructor to substantiate the reason for the absence. This documentation must be provided within three business days of the last date of the absence. Failure to notify and/or document properly will result in an unexcused absence.
IMPORTANT WARNINGS:
1) Retroactive medical excuses (a medical excuse for a condition that occurred in the past) will NOT be considered for an excused absence.
2) Falsification of documentation is a violation of the Honor Code.
3) A medical note for an acute condition excuses you from an absence, not for poor performance. Specifically, if you show up to class, you must take the quiz/exam given, and the grade is yours to keep.
4) An absence for a non-acute medical service does not constitute an excused absence. Therefore, if the student provides a simple note from Student Health Services only indicating that the student had an appointment (but not indicating a date when the student may return to school and not indicating that the condition limits their ability to attend school), I will assume that the absence was for a non-acute medical service, and it will not be excused.
5) As per the student rules, students must let me know before the quiz/exam if they expect to miss a quiz/exam due to a planned university-excused activity.

chunks.title:
Optional Course Information Items
Technology Support
chunks.content:
Technology Support – For technical support related to Canvas, contact the Office of Academic Innovations at aihelp@tamu.edu
For other technical support, contact Help Desk Central at helpdesk@tamu.edu

chunks.title: AI Text Generator Usage Policy
chunks.content:
Al text generators (ChatGPT, Google Bard, etc.) should not be used for any work for this class without the explicit permission of the instructor and appropriate attribution. This includes, but is not limited to:
• Creating or revising drafts
• Editing your work
• Reviewing a peer's work
Use of Al text generators in this manner could constitute plagiarism and cheating under Student Rule 20. (This excludes pre-existing software additions such as spelling and grammar checkers, which are acceptable.) You may contact your instructor if you have questions.
